perturbation.original_text: Contractor agrees to be compensated by the Company as follows: i. Fee The Company will pay Contractor $150,000 annually for the first full year to be adjusted proportionately with increases commensurate and at the same time with those awarded to the CEO and COO of the Company during the term of this contract.
perturbation.changed_text: The consultant will be compensated by the Company as follows: i. Fee The Company will pay consultant $100,000 annually for the first full year to be adjusted at the discretion of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Contractor' is changed to 'consultant,' and the compensation is changed from $150,000 to $100,000, and the adjustment is based on the discretion of the Company, creating a direct contradiction with the initial agreement of $150,000. Later, the agreement still refers to the independent contractor as 'contractor' with the pay mentioned earlier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the first year only a Payment of $25,000 of the Fee shall be advanced to Contractor within the first six months of this Agreement. Additionally, the Contractor will be paid a fee of $10,416.66 per month over the next 12 months. Allowing the contractor to be paid the above agreed upon amount of $150,000 for first twelve months
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by changing 'Contractor' to 'consultant,' reducing the annual compensation from $150,000 to $100,000, and altering the adjustment terms. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which specifies that the 'Contractor' receives an agreed-upon amount of $150,000 for the first twelve months. This change creates a clear and direct conflict in the compensation terms, rendering the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor is, and will remain, an independent contractor in with our relationship to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The consultant is considered an advisor to the Company, but the exact nature of this professional relationship will be determined on a per-project basis.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'independent contractor' is replaced with 'advisor,' and the explicit designation of 'independent contractor' is made ambiguous, creating confusion about the legal status of the contractor. In the original text, there's a clarification of the legal position.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor acknowledges and agrees that am not an employee of Company for any purpose whatsoever, but an independent contractor. Company is interested only in the results obtained by Contractor and I shall have sole control of the manner and means of performance under this Agreement. Company shall not have the right to require Contractor to do anything which would jeopardize the relationship of independent contractor between Company and Contractor. All expenses and disbursements incurred by Contractor in connection with this Agreement shall be borne wholly and completely by Contractor. Contractor does not have, nor shall Contractor hold itself out as having, any right, power or authority to create any contract or obligation, either express or implied, on behalf of, in the name of, or binding upon Company, unless Company shall consent thereto in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the relationship as an 'independent contractor.' The changed text replaces this with 'advisor' and makes the relationship's nature 'determined on a per-project basis.' This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which relies on the specific legal status of 'independent contractor' and grants the contractor sole control. The changed text undermines this status, creating ambiguity and a strong conflict about the contractor's role and independence.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor agrees to indemnify, defend and hold the Company harmless from any liability for, or assessment of, any claims or penalties with respect to such withholding taxes, labor or employment requirements, including any liability for, or assessment of, withholding taxes imposed on the Company by the relevant taxing authorities with respect to any compensation paid to Contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: The consultant is responsible for any personal income taxes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states the independent contractor is responsible for all withholding taxes and indemnifies the company. The changed text only mentions personal income taxes, omitting other potential liabilities and potentially contradicting the broader indemnity clause. Section C is being contradicted as the consultant is now the one being reffered instead of the contractor and the original one required the indemnification, meaning that the consultant wouldn't provide such thing and the original one isn't in charge anymore
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall not be responsible for withholding taxes with respect to our compensation. Contractor agrees to indemnify, defend and hold the Company harmless from any liability for, or assessment of, any claims or penalties with respect to such withholding taxes, labor or employment requirements, including any liability for, or assessment of, withholding taxes imposed on the Company by the relevant taxing authorities with respect to any compensation paid to Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text state that the Contractor is responsible for withholding taxes and indemnifies the company. The changed text replaces 'Contractor' with 'consultant' and narrows the responsibility to only 'personal income taxes,' omitting the broader indemnity clause and the responsibilities related to withholding taxes, which directly contradicts the original agreement regarding the contractor's responsibility as stated in the contradicted text. This contradiction is significant and would create confusion regarding which party is responsible for withholding taxes and indemnification.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor understands that this Agreement shall not render Contractor as an employee, partner, or joint venture with the Company for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The advisor acknowledges that this contract does not establish a formal employer-employee relationship; however, the Company reserves the right to direct the advisor's work.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the contractor is not an employee. The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating that the company reserves the right to direct the advisor's work, blurring the lines between an independent contractor and an employee. This is contradictory in section E where it is reffered to the contractor that the agreement doesn't mean he is an employee, when at the start we changed it to advisor with right to direct the work.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor understands that this Agreement shall not render Contractor as an employee, partner, or joint venture with the Company for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the contractor is not an employee. The changed text, while stating no formal employer-employee relationship, introduces the right to direct the advisor's work, creating a contradiction. This conflicts with the contradicted text, which reaffirms the 'Contractor' is not an employee. The change introduces employee like attributes to the contractor, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor understands that the initial term of this Agreement shall be for two (2) years. However, regardless of the term of this Agreement, Contractor understands that the Company may terminate this Agreement for any reason at any time with 10 days prior written notice to Contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: The initial project duration for the consultant is estimated at six months, with potential extensions as mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specifies a two-year term, while the modified text introduces a six-month project duration with potential extensions, creating a contradiction about the agreement's duration. It has in-text contradiction due to the original text clearly stated to a contractor to be for two years in I.F.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor understands that the initial term of this Agreement shall be for two (2) years. However, regardless of the term of this Agreement, Contractor understands that the Company may terminate this Agreement for any reason at any time with 10 days prior written notice to Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The original text, which is also the contradicted text, states the agreement is for two years. The changed text modifies this to state the project duration is six months, creating a direct conflict in the specified duration of the agreement. The change creates a strong in-text contradiction because the original duration is no longer valid, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: As consideration for entering into this Agreement with the Company, and the Company providing Contractor compensation for our services, Contractor agrees not to compete with the Company as follows: A. During The Time Contractor is Working for the Company . Contractor agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not interfere with the business of the Company in any manner.
perturbation.changed_text: Recognizing the Company's proprietary interests, the independent professional agrees to not engage in conflicting projects for the duration of their engagement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text enforces a non-compete clause. The modified text only restricts engagement in 'conflicting projects,' potentially allowing competition outside those specific projects, which is a contradiction to the first non competition agreement in part V. In section A of part V, it refers the contradiction as it is not specific about 'conflicting project' but 'not interfere with the business'
perturbation.contradicted_text: During The Time Contractor is Working for the Company . Contractor agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not interfere with the business of the Company in any manner. Particularly, but without limitation, Contractor agrees to refrain from planning or organizing a competitive business during the time Contractor is working for the Company. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not have any proprietary interest in any competitive business except for an interest of less than five percent (5%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly-held corporation, meaning a corporation whose outstanding shares are owned by one hundred (100) or more shareholders. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not solicit customers of the Company for any purpose whatsoever. In addition, Contractor represents and warrant that Contractor does not presently perform or intend to perform, during the term of this Agreement, consulting or other services for, or engage in or intend to engage in an employment relationship with, companies and / or individuals who have businesses or proposed businesses in any way involve products or services which would be competitive with the Company’s products or services, or those products or services proposed or in development by the Company during the term of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text explicitly state that the contractor will not interfere with the business of the company in any manner during their engagement. The changed text narrows this restriction to only 'conflicting projects'. This creates a strong contradiction because the changed text permits actions that would be considered interfering with the business, as described in the contradicted text, as long as they don't fall under the definition of 'conflicting projects'. Therefore, the change introduces a weaker, more limited restriction compared to the original and contradicted sections, making them incompatible.
